tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"total_count":14,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3874817319,"number":55374,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[QUERY] Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants package - do you plan to deprecate it ?","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants

### Query/Question

Hi everyone, I'm a developer at DevExpress.  
We have functionality that allows [integrating assistants into the Blazor Chat](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/DevExpress.AIIntegration.Blazor.Chat.DxAIChat#ai-service-assistants).

We also support [Azure Open AI Assistant use](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/foundry-models/concepts/models-sold-directly-by-azure?view=foundry-classic&tabs=global-standard-aoai%2Cglobal-standard&pivots=azure-openai#assistants-preview). Our solution is built using the <https://www.nuget.org/packages/OpenAI> nuget package.

However, OpenAI plans to deprecate the Assistants API on **August 26, 2026** — details here: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/migration>. They recommend migrating to the Responses API.
We are deciding where to migrate, and we want to understand what will happen to Azure AI Assistants in general. And what are the differences between Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants and Azure.AI.OpenAI.

I have a few questions regarding the older NuGet package <https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants/1.0.0-beta.4>.  
Am I correct in assuming that this package is deprecated and that you recommend using <https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.OpenAI/2.8.0-beta.1> instead?
Will you deprecate Assistants in Azure?  And if so, will there be a guide on how to migrate existing assistants?


### Environment

Unable to get tools for Hosted Agents in Foundry","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects (1.2.0-beta.5), Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI (1.0.0-beta.5)

### Describe the bug

I have a hosted agent in Foundry. That hosted agent has some tools. When I try to get the list of tools associated with that hosted agent, I am not getting any tools back.

However, this works with a prompt agent. I created a prompt agent and added web search tool to it. When I list the tools associated with the prompt agent, I get the tools list with web search tool in it.

### Expected behavior

For both hosted and prompt agents in Foundry, I should be able to get the list of tools attached/associated with that agent. Currently it only works with prompt agents.

### Actual behavior

For hosted agents, tools are not returned.

### Reproduction Steps

```
var projectEndpoint = "https://my-foundry.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/my-project";
var agentId = "my-hosted-agent"

var projectClient = new AIProjectClient(
		endpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint),
		tokenProvider: new DefaultAzureCredential());
var agentRecordResult = await projectClient.Agents.GetAgentAsync(agentId);
var response = agentRecordResult.GetRawResponse().Content.ToString();
```

For hosted agents, the response looks like this:

```
{
  "object": "agent",
  "id": ""my-hosted-agent",
  "name": ""my-hosted-agent",
  "versions": {
    "latest": {
      "metadata": {},
      "object": "agent.version",
      "id": ""my-hosted-agent:4",
      "name": ""my-hosted-agent",
      "version": "4",
      "description": "My Agent",
      "created_at": 1771616539,
      "definition": {
        "kind": "hosted",
        "container_protocol_versions": [
          {
            "protocol": "responses",
            "version": "v1"
          }
        ],
        "cpu": "1",
        "memory": "2Gi",
        "environment_variables": {
          "AzureOpenAI__DeploymentName": "gpt-4.1",
          "AzureOpenAI__Endpoint": "https://my-foundry.cognitiveservices.azure.com/"
        },
        "image": "myacr.azurecr.io/my-hosted-agent:v4"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

For prompt agent, the output of the same code looks like this:

```
{
  "object": "agent",
  "id": "my-prompt-agent",
  "name": "my-prompt-agent",
  "versions": {
    "latest": {
      "metadata": {
        "logo": "Avatar_Default.svg",
        "description": "",
        "modified_at": "1771954556"
      },
      "object": "agent.version",
      "id": "my-prompt-agent:3",
      "name": "my-prompt-agent",
      "version": "3",
      "description": "",
      "created_at": 1771954556,
      "definition": {
        "kind": "prompt",
        "model": "gpt-4.1",
        "instructions": "",
        "tools": [
          {
            "type": "web_search_preview"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```



### Environment

Hosting Platform: Azure Foundry

vatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nick863/subscriptions"}],"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7tiILT"},{"id":3712790964,"number":54411,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[BUG] - MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

The class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.

The `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.

Here are some examples in python:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48

```python
    tool = MCPTool(
        server_label="api-specs",
        server_url="https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp",
        require_approval="always",
        project_connection_id=os.environ["MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID"],
    )
```

I believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.

### Expected behavior

Be able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.

### Actual behavior

Impossible to set the the property in any shape or form.

### Reproduction Steps

```csharp
new MCPToolDefinition("MCP-Server-Label", "MCP-Remote-URL")
```

MCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property

### Environment

OS: Windows 11 25H2 26200.6584
dotnet: 10.0.100

VisualStudioCredential failing to retrieve token with scope ai.azure.com/.default for new Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.1.0-beta.1

### Describe the bug

Since the latest release of Azure.AI.Projects for Azure Foundry integration VisualStudioCredential within Visual Studio 2022 no longer works.

- VisualStudioCredential authentication failed: Process "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\Common7\IDE\CommonExtensions\Microsoft\Asal\TokenService\Microsoft.Asal.TokenService.exe" has failed with unexpected error: TS003: Error, TS004: Unable to get access token.  'Consent between first party application '04f0c124-f2bc-4f59-8241-bf6df9866bbd' and first party resource '18a66f5f-dbdf-4c17-9dd7-1634712a9cbe' must be configured via preauthorization - applications owned and operated by Microsoft must get approval from the API owner before requesting tokens for that API. Trace ID: 63dae0af-5e56-4ca5-b343-8700c30da500 Correlation ID: 003078b6-5f8a-4fe4-ac79-060af246ea7e Timestamp: 2025-05-25 20:12:25Z
For potential mitigation and additional information go to the following url: 'https://aka.ms/vspreautherror''.

This use to work prior to the new SDK as authentication used https://management.azure.com//.default for scopes. The new SDK uses https://ai.azure.com/.default and this fails with the above message.
I can use Azure CLI Credentials and the application will work as it uses a different ClientID but our development and test environments current rely on DefaultAzureCredentials and Visual Studio with Azure Service Authentication.
This all worked fine prior to the upgrade to the Azure.AI SDKs and the new structure. The root appears to be the VS App GUID not be registered with the Azure ML Services.

1.1.0-beta.1 (2025-05-21) - Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent
1.0.0-beta.9 (2025-05-16) - Azure.AI.Projects

### Expected behavior

As before - VisualStudioCredentials is able to authenticate against https://ai.azure.com/.default using VS App ID when using the latest version of Azure.AI.Projects and Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.

### Actual behavior

Any use of VisualStudioCredentials with the latest Azure.AI.Projects or Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent fails which makes the use of DefaultAzureCredentials difficult in test and development.

### Reproduction Steps

Use Visual Studio (VisualStudioCredentials) and the latest SDKs to access Azure AI Foundry

### Environment

Visual Studio 2022 - Enterprise or Community
.Net 8
1.1.0-beta.1 (2025-05-21) - Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent
1.0.0-beta.9 (2025-05-16) - Azure.AI.Projects","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"simontfoil","id":188108382,"node_id":"U_kgDOCzZOXg","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/188108382?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/simontfoil","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simontfoil/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178447860,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client","name":"Client","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a non-management package","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw"},{"id":1344097830,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Azure.Identity","name":"Azure.Identity","color":"e99695","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzQ0MDk3ODMw"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. CustomKeys credentials are not serialized correctly","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects 1.0.0-beta.11

### Describe the bug

When I use the SDK to get CustomKeys connections, the Credentials part is not serialized correctly. The actual response is compatible with the `DeserializeCustomCredential` method.

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/src/Generated/Models/CustomCredential.Serialization.cs#L71

There is no "keys" properties in the response inside the "credentials" object.

The raw response from the api looks like:
```json
{
  "name" : "**omit**",
  "id" : "**omit**",
  "type" : "CustomKeys",
  "target" : "_",
  "isDefault" : false,
  "credentials" : {
    "password" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**",
    "type" : "CustomKeys"
  },
  "metadata" : {
    "baseUrl" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**",
    "username" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**"
  }
}
```

The `DeserializeCustomCredential` tries to read the key `credentials.keys` as a nested object to create the `Keys` property of the `CustomCredential.Credentials.Keys`

Something like that I guess:
```json
{
  // omit
  "credentials": {
    "type": "CustomKeys",
    "keys": {
      "[KEY_NAME]": "[KEY_VALUE]"
    }
  }
  // omit
}
```

### Expected behavior

I expect the custom keys defined as "secret" in Azure Foundry available when using the AI Project SDK in the Credentials property of `CustomCredential`.

### Actual behavior

Only the non secret custom keys are available in the metadata property.
Nothing in the Credentials property.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Create a AI Foundry Project
2. Add a Custom key connection "test"
3. Add at least one secret key
4. use `AiProjectClient.Connections.GetConnectionAsync("test", includeCredentials: true)`
5. Try to access to `ClientResult<ConnectionProperties>.Value.Credentials.Keys`
6. See `null`

### Environment

Windows 10, Dotnet 9 

.NET SDK:
 Version:           9.0.304
 Commit:            f12f5f689e

.NET workloads installed:
There are no installed workloads to display.
Configured to use loose manifests when installing new manifests.

  Architecture: x64
  Commit:       aae90fa090

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** Two related bugs in `Azure.AI.Projects` 2.0.0-beta.2 affecting Azure-specific agent tool calls (`openapi_call`) in the Responses and Conversations APIs:
1. **Stateless multi-turn replay fails** — `openapi_call` items from `ResponseResult.OutputItems` are rejected (HTTP 400) when sent back as `InputItems` in a follow-up request.
2. **Dual discriminator** — the same OpenAPI tool call surfaces as `openapi_call` in the Responses API output but as undocumented `remote_function_call` (with `"label": "remote_openapi"`) in the Conversations API, causing all items to deserialize as `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Analysis details</summary>

### Issue breakdown

**Issue 1 — Stateless replay (HTTP 400)**
- `ProjectResponsesClient.CreateResponse` succeeds for an initial request that triggers an OpenAPI tool call.
- The resulting `openapi_call` item in `OutputItems` cannot be replayed as an `InputItem` — the server rejects it with `invalid_value` for `openapi_call`.
- Workaround: use `PreviousResponseId` or the Conversations API with server-managed state.

**Issue 2 — Dual discriminator**
- Responses API output uses `"type": "openapi_call"` (present in the published Foundry OpenAPI spec).
- Conversations API (`GetProjectConversationItemsAsync`) returns `"type": "remote_function_call"` with `"label": "remote_openapi"` — not in the published spec and not handled by `AgentResponseItem`'s deserializer.
- Result: all conversation items fall through to `UnknownAgentResponseItem`.

### Root causes to investigate
- [ ] Service-side: does the Foundry Responses API need to accept `openapi_call`/`openapi_call_output` as valid input types for stateless replay?
- [ ] Service-side: does the Conversations API need to align its `type` discriminator with the Responses API (`openapi_call` vs `remote_function_call`)?
- [ ] SDK-side: should `AgentResponseItem`'s deserializer add a case for `remote_function_call` (mapping to the appropriate subtype) to unblock users in the meantime?
- [ ] SDK-side: `AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs` already has a case for `openapi_call` — does `remote_function_call` + `"label": "remote_openapi"` need to be mapped to the same type?

### Labels applied
- **Category:** `Client` — the issue involves SDK deserialization behavior and API surface
- **Service:** `AI Projects` — package `Azure.AI.Projects` in `/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/`
- Other labels considered: `AI Agents` (rejected — `Azure.AI.Projects` maps to `AI Projects`, not `AI Agents`)

### Environment
- Windows 11, .NET 10.0
- Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2
- Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1 (transitive)
- OpenAI 2.9.1 (transitive)
